narrative:
Manuel Akanji is a Centre-Back for Nigeria. His overall rating stands at 7.33/10, placing him in the top 2% of centre-backs at this tournament. His rating range of 6.94–7.71 reflects a consistent performance level.

At this World Cup, Akanji averages 1.75 tackles and 1.25 interceptions per 90 minutes, demonstrating strong defensive engagement. He also contributes significantly with 4.75 clearances per 90 minutes. His offensive output, however, is modest, with an xG of 0.067 and xA of 0.05 per 90 minutes.

Akanji serves as a crucial defensive anchor for his national team, having played 360 World Cup minutes. Scouts and fans should continue to observe his exceptional defensive work rate. His ability to break up play and clear danger remains a key attribute in Nigeria's campaign.
